TEXT.citation: Early, R. J., & Whitehorn, J. (2003). One hundred Paiwan texts. Pacific Linguistics, Research School of Pacific and Asian Studies, The Australian National University.
TEXT.BibTeX_citation: @book{100paiwantexts, author = {Robert Early and John Whitehorn}, title = {One Hundred Paiwan Texts}, year = {2003}, publisher = {Pacific Linguistics, Research School of Pacific and Asian Studies, The Australian National University}}
TEXT.source: qaruqaruman kata ḍail (Tjauvaɫi [or Tjavuaɫi] village, p.251)

S.FORM: izua qaruqaruman kata ḍail a mareqali.
S.PHON: izua qaruqaruman kata ɖaiɭ a marəqaɭi.
S.FORM: izua qaruqaruman kata drail a mareqali.
S.PHON: izua qaruqaruman kata ɖaiɭ a marəqaɭi.
S.TRANSL: There were two friends, the ant-eater and the monkey.

S.FORM: aza ḍail: "ari kikasivi, qalian," ayain a qaruqaruman.
S.PHON: aza ɖaiɭ: "ari kikasivi, qaɭian," ajain a qaruqaruman.
S.FORM: aza drail: "ari kikasivi, qalian," ayain a qaruqaruman.
S.PHON: aza ɖaiɭ: "ari kikasivi, qaɭian," ajain a qaruqaruman.
S.TRANSL: The monkey said to the ant-eater: “Come on, friend, let’s go and collect wood.”

S.FORM: qau vaik a kikasiw.
S.PHON: qau vaik a kikasiw.
S.FORM: qau vaik a kikasiw.
S.PHON: qau vaik a kikasiw.
S.TRANSL: They went to collect wood.

S.FORM: qau matsaqu aravats a ḍail a kikasiw.
S.PHON: qau maʦaqu aravaʦ a ɖaiɭ a kikasiw.
S.FORM: qau macaqu aravac a drail a kikasiw.
S.PHON: qau maʦaqu aravaʦ a ɖaiɭ a kikasiw.
S.TRANSL: The monkey was very good at collecting wood;

S.FORM: aza qaruqaruman inika matsaqu a kikasiw.
S.PHON: aza qaruqaruman inika maʦaqu a kikasiw.
S.FORM: aza qaruqaruman inika macaqu a kikasiw.
S.PHON: aza qaruqaruman inika maʦaqu a kikasiw.
S.TRANSL: but the ant-eater wasn’t.

S.FORM: "tja kudain nu kikasiw itjen?" aya a qaruqaruman tua ḍail a kivadaq.
S.PHON: "ca kudain nu kikasiw icən" aja a qaruqaruman tua ɖaiɭ a kivadaq.
S.FORM: "tja kudain nu kikasiw itjen?" aya a qaruqaruman tua drail a kivadaq.
S.PHON: "ʦa kudain nu kikasiw iʦən" aja a qaruqaruman tua ɖaiɭ a kivadaq.
S.TRANSL: “How do we collect wood?” the ant-eater asked the monkey.

S.FORM: "tja kiluḍayan, qau matsaqu itjen a kikasiw," ayain nua ḍail.
S.PHON: "ca kiɭuɖajan, qau maʦaqu icən a kikasiw," ajain nua ɖaiɭ.
S.FORM: "tja kiludrayan, qau macaqu itjen a kikasiw," ayain nua drail.
S.PHON: "ʦa kiɭuɖajan, qau maʦaqu iʦən a kikasiw," ajain nua ɖaiɭ.
S.TRANSL: “We roll around on it(?). Then we are good at collecting wood,” said the monkey.

S.FORM: qau kiluḍayan nua qaruqaruman a maka pavavaw a ɫaviavia.
S.PHON: qau kiɭuɖajan nua qaruqaruman a maka pavavaw a ʎaviavia.
S.FORM: qau kiludrayan nua qaruqaruman a maka pavavaw a ljaviavia.
S.PHON: qau kiɭuɖajan nua qaruqaruman a maka pavavaw a ʎaviavia.
S.TRANSL: So the ant-eater rolled around (on the wood (?)) on the grass.

S.FORM: inika putjaumaɫ.
S.PHON: inika pucaumaʎ.
S.FORM: inika putjaumalj.
S.PHON: inika puʦaumaʎ.
S.TRANSL: He didn’t follow instructions.

S.FORM: qau vaik i umaq tiamadju.
S.PHON: qau vaik i umaq tiamaɉu.
S.FORM: qau vaik i umaq tiamadju.
S.PHON: qau vaik i umaq tiamaɉu.
S.TRANSL: And they went home.

S.FORM: ka maɫia, "ari kigang itjen," aya a qaruqaruman tua ḍail.
S.PHON: ka maʎia, "ari kigaŋ icən," aja a qaruqaruman tua ɖaiɭ.
S.FORM: ka maljia, "ari kigang itjen," aya a qaruqaruman tua drail.
S.PHON: ka maʎia, "ari kigaŋ iʦən," aja a qaruqaruman tua ɖaiɭ.
S.TRANSL: Next day, “Come on, we’re going to collect crabs,” said the ant-eater to the monkey.

S.FORM: qau vaik a kigang.
S.PHON: qau vaik a kigaŋ.
S.FORM: qau vaik a kigang.
S.PHON: qau vaik a kigaŋ.
S.TRANSL: So they went to get crabs.

S.FORM: qau inika matsaqu a kigang a ḍail.
S.PHON: qau inika maʦaqu a kigaŋ a ɖaiɭ.
S.FORM: qau inika macaqu a kigang a drail.
S.PHON: qau inika maʦaqu a kigaŋ a ɖaiɭ.
S.TRANSL: The monkey wasn’t good at collecting crabs.

S.FORM: "tja kudain nu kigang itjen?" aya a ḍail a kivadaq tua qaruqaruman.
S.PHON: "ca kudain nu kigaŋ icən" aja a ɖaiɭ a kivadaq tua qaruqaruman.
S.FORM: "tja kudain nu kigang itjen?" aya a drail a kivadaq tua qaruqaruman.
S.PHON: "ʦa kudain nu kigaŋ iʦən" aja a ɖaiɭ a kivadaq tua qaruqaruman.
S.TRANSL: “How do we collect crabs?” the monkey asked the ant-eater.

S.FORM: qau "tja tjuqtjuqen a tja lima," ayain nua qaruqaruman a tjemumaɫ.
S.PHON: qau "ca cuqcuqən a ca ɭima," ajain nua qaruqaruman a cəmumaʎ.
S.FORM: qau "tja tjuqtjuqen a tja lima," ayain nua qaruqaruman a tjemumalj.
S.PHON: qau "ʦa ʦuqʦuqən a ʦa ɭima," ajain nua qaruqaruman a ʦəmumaʎ.
S.TRANSL: “We hit our arms,” the ant-eater told the monkey.

S.FORM: qau tjuqtjuqen a lima nua ḍail.
S.PHON: qau cuqcuqən a ɭima nua ɖaiɭ.
S.FORM: qau tjuqtjuqen a lima nua drail.
S.PHON: qau ʦuqʦuqən a ɭima nua ɖaiɭ.
S.TRANSL: The monkey hit his arms;

S.FORM: ɫakua saqetju sakamaya.
S.PHON: ʎakua saqəcu sakamaja.
S.FORM: ljakua saqetju sakamaya.
S.PHON: ʎakua saqəʦu sakamaja.
S.TRANSL: but it only hurt.

S.FORM: inika matsaqu a kigang a ḍail.
S.PHON: inika maʦaqu a kigaŋ a ɖaiɭ.
S.FORM: inika macaqu a kigang a drail.
S.PHON: inika maʦaqu a kigaŋ a ɖaiɭ.
S.TRANSL: The monkey wasn’t any good at collecting crabs.

S.FORM: qau "temapi itjen tua vasa," aya tiamadju.
S.PHON: qau "təmapi icən tua vasa," aja tiamaɉu.
S.FORM: qau "temapi itjen tua vasa," aya tiamadju.
S.PHON: qau "təmapi iʦən tua vasa," aja tiamaɉu.
S.TRANSL: “We’ll cook some taro in an earth oven,” they said.

S.FORM: qau temapi; penuɫat a tinapi.
S.PHON: qau təmapi; pənuʎat a tinapi.
S.FORM: qau temapi; penuljat a tinapi.
S.PHON: qau təmapi; pənuʎat a tinapi.
S.TRANSL: So they made an earth-oven; all their taro was cooked (?).

S.FORM: "ari, mavanaw itjen a sema djanaw," aya.
S.PHON: "ari, mavanaw icən a səma ɉanaw," aja.
S.FORM: "ari, mavanaw itjen a sema djanaw," aya.
S.PHON: "ari, mavanaw iʦən a səma ɉanaw," aja.
S.TRANSL: “Come on, we’ll bathe in the pool,” they said.

S.FORM: vaik a mavanaw.
S.PHON: vaik a mavanaw.
S.FORM: vaik a mavanaw.
S.PHON: vaik a mavanaw.
S.TRANSL: They went to bathe.

S.FORM: qau "pare ledeledepi; tima a tsuatsuay?" aya tiamadju.
S.PHON: qau "parə ɭədəɭədəpi; tima a ʦuaʦuaj" aja tiamaɉu.
S.FORM: qau "pare ledeledepi; tima a cuacuay?" aya tiamadju.
S.PHON: qau "parə ɭədəɭədəpi; tima a ʦuaʦuaj" aja tiamaɉu.
S.TRANSL: “Let’s both dive in, and see who can stay under longest,” they said.

S.FORM: "nu maitazua, tiakenay anan," aya a ḍail.
S.PHON: "nu maitazua, tiakənaj anan," aja a ɖaiɭ.
S.FORM: "nu maitazua, tiakenay anan," aya a drail.
S.PHON: "nu maitazua, tiakənaj anan," aja a ɖaiɭ.
S.TRANSL: “All right; I’ll go first,” said the monkey.

S.FORM: qau lemdep a ḍail.
S.PHON: qau ɭəmdəp a ɖaiɭ.
S.FORM: qau lemdep a drail.
S.PHON: qau ɭəmdəp a ɖaiɭ.
S.TRANSL: The monkey dived in;

S.FORM: inika tsuatsuay tjekatsauan.
S.PHON: inika ʦuaʦuaj cəkaʦauan.
S.FORM: inika cuacuay tjekacauan.
S.PHON: inika ʦuaʦuaj ʦəkaʦauan.
S.TRANSL: and quite soon came out again.

S.FORM: "tiakenay anga," aya qaruqaruman.
S.PHON: "tiakənaj aŋa," aja qaruqaruman.
S.FORM: "tiakenay anga," aya qaruqaruman.
S.PHON: "tiakənaj aŋa," aja qaruqaruman.
S.TRANSL: “Now me,” said the ant-eater.

S.FORM: qau lemdep a qaruqaruman.
S.PHON: qau ɭəmdəp a qaruqaruman.
S.FORM: qau lemdep a qaruqaruman.
S.PHON: qau ɭəmdəp a qaruqaruman.
S.TRANSL: The ant-eater dived in;

S.FORM: sa kemali a ɫetaladj tua qipu; padjaɫun tua tinapi niamadju.
S.PHON: sa kəmaɭi a ʎətaɭaɉ tua qipu; paɉaʎun tua tinapi niamaɉu.
S.FORM: sa kemali a ljetaladj tua qipu; padjaljun tua tinapi niamadju.
S.PHON: sa kəmaɭi a ʎətaɭaɉ tua qipu; paɉaʎun tua tinapi niamaɉu.
S.TRANSL: and dug into the ground, and reached the taros they had cooked.

S.FORM: qau kanen a kuḍakuḍalan a tinapi.
S.PHON: qau kanən a kuɖakuɖaɭan a tinapi.
S.FORM: qau kanen a kudrakudralan a tinapi.
S.PHON: qau kanən a kuɖakuɖaɭan a tinapi.
S.TRANSL: He ate the big taros;

S.FORM: qau tjekatsauan a qaruqaruman.
S.PHON: qau cəkaʦauan a qaruqaruman.
S.FORM: qau tjekacauan a qaruqaruman.
S.PHON: qau ʦəkaʦauan a qaruqaruman.
S.TRANSL: and then the ant-eater came out of the water.

S.FORM: qau "tja kanaw a tja tinapi," aya.
S.PHON: qau "ca kanaw a ca tinapi," aja.
S.FORM: qau "tja kanaw a tja tinapi," aya.
S.PHON: qau "ʦa kanaw a ʦa tinapi," aja.
S.TRANSL: “We’ll eat what we’ve cooked,” they said;

S.FORM: sutapian; qau nekanga a kuḍakuḍalan.
S.PHON: sutapian; qau nəkaŋa a kuɖakuɖaɭan.
S.FORM: sutapian; qau nekanga a kudrakudralan.
S.PHON: sutapian; qau nəkaŋa a kuɖakuɖaɭan.
S.TRANSL: and uncovered the earth-oven. There were no big taros.

S.FORM: qau kanen anga azua keḍikeḍian.
S.PHON: qau kanən aŋa azua kəɖikəɖian.
S.FORM: qau kanen anga azua kedrikedrian.
S.PHON: qau kanən aŋa azua kəɖikəɖian.
S.TRANSL: So they just ate the small ones.

S.FORM: ka makakan, "putsaqi itjen, tuki tima kuḍakuḍal a tsaqi.
S.PHON: ka makakan, "puʦaqi icən, tuki tima kuɖakuɖaɭ a ʦaqi.
S.FORM: ka makakan, "pucaqi itjen, tuki tima kudrakudral a caqi.
S.PHON: ka makakan, "puʦaqi iʦən, tuki tima kuɖakuɖaɭ a ʦaqi.
S.TRANSL: When they had finished eating the two of them said: “We’ll defecate, and see who has the bigger faeces.

S.FORM: azua tjakuḍal a tsaqi, avan nu nakeman tua tinapi a kuḍakuḍalan," aya tiamadju a maḍusa.
S.PHON: azua cakuɖaɭ a ʦaqi, avan nu nakəman tua tinapi a kuɖakuɖaɭan," aja tiamaɉu a maɖusa.
S.FORM: azua tjakudral a caqi, avan nu nakeman tua tinapi a kudrakudralan," aya tiamadju a madrusa.
S.PHON: azua ʦakuɖaɭ a ʦaqi, avan nu nakəman tua tinapi a kuɖakuɖaɭan," aja tiamaɉu a maɖusa.
S.TRANSL: The one with the bigger faeces will be the one who has eaten the big cooked taros.”

S.FORM: qau putsaqi tiamadju.
S.PHON: qau puʦaqi tiamaɉu.
S.FORM: qau pucaqi tiamadju.
S.PHON: qau puʦaqi tiamaɉu.
S.TRANSL: They defecated,

S.FORM: tjakuḍal a tsaqi nua qaruqaruman.
S.PHON: cakuɖaɭ a ʦaqi nua qaruqaruman.
S.FORM: tjakudral a caqi nua qaruqaruman.
S.PHON: ʦakuɖaɭ a ʦaqi nua qaruqaruman.
S.TRANSL: and the ant-eater’s faeces was bigger.

S.FORM: qau vetseqelen a qaruqaruman nua ḍail.
S.PHON: qau vəʦəqəɭən a qaruqaruman nua ɖaiɭ.
S.FORM: qau veceqelen a qaruqaruman nua drail.
S.PHON: qau vəʦəqəɭən a qaruqaruman nua ɖaiɭ.
S.TRANSL: And the monkey strangled the ant-eater.

S.FORM: qau "paɫaɫama-i," aya tiamadju a maɫavar.
S.PHON: qau "paʎaʎama-i," aja tiamaɉu a maʎavar.
S.FORM: qau "paljaljama-i," aya tiamadju a maljavar.
S.PHON: qau "paʎaʎama-i," aja tiamaɉu a maʎavar.
S.TRANSL: “Let’s set fire to one another,” they decided.

S.FORM: qau "tiakenay isangas," aya ti qaruqaruman.
S.PHON: qau "tiakənaj isaŋas," aja ti qaruqaruman.
S.FORM: qau "tiakenay isangas," aya ti qaruqaruman.
S.PHON: qau "tiakənaj isaŋas," aja ti qaruqaruman.
S.TRANSL: “Me first,” said the ant-eater.

S.FORM: qau ɫamain ti qaruqaruman; inika matsay.
S.PHON: qau ʎamain ti qaruqaruman; inika maʦaj.
S.FORM: qau ljamain ti qaruqaruman; inika macay.
S.PHON: qau ʎamain ti qaruqaruman; inika maʦaj.
S.TRANSL: The (grass round the (OA)) ant-eater was set on fire; but he didn’t die.

S.FORM: "tiaken anga," aya a ḍail.
S.PHON: "tiakən aŋa," aja a ɖaiɭ.
S.FORM: "tiaken anga," aya a drail.
S.PHON: "tiakən aŋa," aja a ɖaiɭ.
S.TRANSL: “Now me,” said the monkey.

S.FORM: qau ɫevavaw ta kasiw.
S.PHON: qau ʎəvavaw ta kasiw.
S.FORM: qau ljevavaw ta kasiw.
S.PHON: qau ʎəvavaw ta kasiw.
S.TRANSL: He went up a tree.

S.FORM: ka ɫamain, matsay a ḍail.
S.PHON: ka ʎamain, maʦaj a ɖaiɭ.
S.FORM: ka ljamain, macay a drail.
S.PHON: ka ʎamain, maʦaj a ɖaiɭ.
S.TRANSL: When he/it was set on fire, the monkey died.

S.FORM: ka matsay a ḍail, kitjen a qalitsi nua ḍail; saka mevaɫut a ḍail.
S.PHON: ka maʦaj a ɖaiɭ, kicən a qaɭiʦi nua ɖaiɭ; saka məvaʎut a ɖaiɭ.
S.FORM: ka macay a drail, kitjen a qalici nua drail; saka mevaljut a drail.
S.PHON: ka maʦaj a ɖaiɭ, kiʦən a qaɭiʦi nua ɖaiɭ; saka məvaʎut a ɖaiɭ.
S.TRANSL: When the monkey had died, [the ant-eater] cut off the monkey’s penis. And the monkey came back to life.

S.FORM: sipakan ni qaruqaruman tua ḍail azua qalitsi.
S.PHON: sipakan ni qaruqaruman tua ɖaiɭ azua qaɭiʦi.
S.FORM: sipakan ni qaruqaruman tua drail azua qalici.
S.PHON: sipakan ni qaruqaruman tua ɖaiɭ azua qaɭiʦi.
S.TRANSL: The ant-eater fed the penis to the monkey.

S.FORM: qau "kemakan ti ḍail tua qalitsi nimadju," semenasenay ti qaruqaruman.
S.PHON: qau "kəmakan ti ɖaiɭ tua qaɭiʦi nimaɉu," səmənasənaj ti qaruqaruman.
S.FORM: qau "kemakan ti drail tua qalici nimadju," semenasenay ti qaruqaruman.
S.PHON: qau "kəmakan ti ɖaiɭ tua qaɭiʦi nimaɉu," səmənasənaj ti qaruqaruman.
S.TRANSL: And the ant-eater sang: “The monkey is eating his penis.”

S.FORM: "anemanema aya sun?" aya a ḍail.
S.PHON: "anəmanəma aja sun" aja a ɖaiɭ.
S.FORM: "anemanema aya sun?" aya a drail.
S.PHON: "anəmanəma aja sun" aja a ɖaiɭ.
S.TRANSL: “What are you saying?” said the monkey.

S.FORM: "kidjavadjavay aken tua se Tjavaliɫuk tua ku kinipangaɫan," aya ti qaruqaruman.
S.PHON: "kiɉavaɉavaj akən tua sə cavaɭiʎuk tua ku kinipaŋaʎan," aja ti qaruqaruman.
S.FORM: "kidjavadjavay aken tua se Tjavaliljuk tua ku kinipangaljan," aya ti qaruqaruman.
S.PHON: "kiɉavaɉavaj akən tua sə ʦavaɭiʎuk tua ku kinipaŋaʎan," aja ti qaruqaruman.
S.TRANSL: “I’m thanking the Tjavaliɫuk people for the share (of meat) they have given me,” said the ant-eater.

S.FORM: sauniuni "qau pai patsuni a nisun," ayain a ḍail nua qaruqaruman.
S.PHON: sauniuni "qau pai paʦuni a nisun," ajain a ɖaiɭ nua qaruqaruman.
S.FORM: sauniuni "qau pai pacuni a nisun," ayain a drail nua qaruqaruman.
S.PHON: sauniuni "qau pai paʦuni a nisun," ajain a ɖaiɭ nua qaruqaruman.
S.TRANSL: Later the ant-eater said to the monkey: “Let’s look at your private parts.”

S.FORM: qau qayamen a nimadju na ḍail; nekanga a qalitsi; kinan anga a nimadju.
S.PHON: qau qajamən a nimaɉu na ɖaiɭ; nəkaŋa a qaɭiʦi; kinan aŋa a nimaɉu.
S.FORM: qau qayamen a nimadju na drail; nekanga a qalici; kinan anga a nimadju.
S.PHON: qau qajamən a nimaɉu na ɖaiɭ; nəkaŋa a qaɭiʦi; kinan aŋa a nimaɉu.
S.TRANSL: The monkey had a look, and he had no penis; it had been eaten.
